title: Kardemummabullar
subtitle: Swedish cardamom buns with no cinnamon filling
excerpt: Kardemummabullar are the more aromatic sibling of kanelbullar: cardamom goes into both the dough and the filling, with no cinnamon. The result is fragrant and floral, the flavour entirely from the spice.
sourceNotes: Kardemummabullar appear in Swedish baking alongside kanelbullar, distinguished by using cardamom in the filling rather than (or in addition to) cinnamon. The recipe became more widely known outside Scandinavia in the 2010s, though the bun itself is a fixture of Swedish cafés and home baking. The enriched-dough base follows the same pattern as kanelbullar; the distinction lies entirely in the filling.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked buns individually once fully cooled. Warm in a 170°C oven for 8 minutes from frozen.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Shape and prove overnight in the refrigerator; bake the following morning after 45 minutes at room temperature.

glossaryTerms.definition: Cardamom loses its floral top notes quickly after grinding. Pre-ground cardamom from a jar is significantly weaker than the same weight of seeds ground from pods on the day. For buns where cardamom is the central flavour, crack open green pods, remove the seeds, and grind them in a spice grinder or with a pestle and mortar.

content.text: Where kanelbullar have warmth from cinnamon, kardemummabullar are all floral lift. The filling is butter, brown sugar, and ground cardamom; the dough carries cardamom too. The key is using
: the spice decays fast and pre-ground from the jar produces a pale version of the real thing.
Combine the flour, yeast, salt, sugar, and cardamom. Add the warm milk and soft butter and mix to a dough. Knead for 10 minutes by hand until smooth, or 6 minutes in a stand mixer with a dough hook.
Place in an oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and leave until doubled. About 60 minutes in a warm kitchen.

Mix the filling to a smooth paste. Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface to a 40×50 cm rectangle. Spread the filling evenly to within 1 cm of the edges. Fold the dough lengthwise into thirds, pressing the layers together gently. Cut into 16 strips of about 2.5 cm each. Twist each strip twice and coil it into a round, tucking the end underneath, or use the knotted method from the kanelbullar recipe. Place on lined baking trays with 5 cm between each.

Cover loosely and leave for 30 minutes until slightly puffed. Preheat the oven to 220°C conventional. Brush with beaten egg and scatter pearl sugar over the tops. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until deep gold on the peaks and set in the centre. Cool on a rack; eat warm.
